symptom: diamond-shaped lesions on leaves with gray center and brown margins; neck rot in severe cases
impact: reduces photosynthesis and can cause severe yield loss due to panicle damage
treatment: spray Tricyclazole 0.6 g/L at early stage
apply fungicide at first appearance of lesions
if_then_logic: if high humidity and dense planting then high risk of blast infection
if excess nitrogen applied then blast severity increases
content: Rice blast is a fungal disease caused by Magnaporthe oryzae. It appears as diamond-shaped lesions on leaves and can progress to neck rot, severely reducing yield. It is favored by high humidity, moderate temperatures, dense planting, and excess nitrogen fertilization.

symptom: yellowing and drying of leaf margins starting from tips; water-soaked streaks
impact: reduces leaf area and photosynthesis, leading to lower grain filling
if_then_logic: if high humidity and standing water then BLB risk increases
if leaves are damaged by wind then infection spreads faster
content: Bacterial leaf blight in rice causes yellowing from leaf tips and spreads downward. It is common in high humidity and warm temperatures, especially in fields with standing water. The disease reduces photosynthesis and grain yield.

symptom: small brown circular spots on leaves; may enlarge and coalesce
cause: fungal infection (Bipolaris oryzae) often associated with nutrient deficiency
impact: reduces leaf area and plant vigor, leading to lower yield
if_then_logic: if soil fertility is low then brown spot risk increases
if drought stress occurs then severity increases
content: Brown spot disease in rice appears as small brown lesions on leaves and is often linked to poor soil fertility and drought stress. It reduces plant vigor and yield, especially under nutrient-deficient conditions.

symptom: oval or irregular green-gray lesions on leaf sheath near water level
impact: reduces photosynthetic area and weakens plant structure
if_then_logic: if dense canopy and high humidity then sheath blight risk increases
content: Sheath blight affects rice plants in dense and humid conditions. It starts near the water line and spreads upward, reducing plant strength and yield. Proper spacing and nitrogen management help reduce risk.

symptom: uniform yellowing of older leaves, poor tillering
prevention: apply nitrogen fertilizer in split doses
treatment: apply urea fertilizer as per recommendation
if_then_logic: if leaves turn yellow uniformly then nitrogen deficiency likely
content: Nitrogen deficiency in rice causes yellowing of older leaves and reduced tillering. It directly impacts plant growth and yield. Proper nitrogen management is essential for maintaining productivity.
